_comment: Canonical model tiers. Paseo provider strings = 'opencode/' + opencode model id for opencode-routed models. subscription-high.neverSubagent: those models must NEVER be deployed as subagents under any circumstances.

model_strengths._note: Authoritative per-model strengths the orchestrator consults to pick the best-fit candidate from a role's option pool. Sourced from Sam's 2026-06 model briefing plus the pricing block below; supersedes the earlier derived seed. Benchmark figures are vendor or independent per the briefing.
model_strengths.deepseek-v4-flash: Efficient open frontier-ish coding/agent (284B MoE / 13B active, MIT, 1M ctx). Low-latency, cheapest tier; the realistic V4 self-host target (160GB FP4+FP8, two H200s). Best for high-volume mechanical work; lighter and less careful than V4 Pro.
model_strengths.mimo-v2.5: Xiaomi unified omnimodal (310B sparse MoE / 15B active). Native visual+audio; matches Gemini 3 Pro on video and Sonnet 4.6 on multimodal agentic, at half V2.5-Pro cost. Cheapest tier. Best cheap multimodal all-rounder and high-volume default.
model_strengths.deepseek-v4-pro: Open frontier-tier (1.6T MoE / 49B active, MIT, 1M ctx). 80.6% SWE-bench Verified (highest open-weights, tied Gemini 3.1 Pro), 3206 Codeforces, 95.2 HMMT; Anthropic-API format. Best open coding/math at frontier for the price. Caveat: drops to 66% MRCR at full 1M, sweet spot 128K-512K.
model_strengths.mimo-v2.5-pro: Xiaomi flagship reasoning/long-horizon (1.02T MoE / 42B active, MIT). Top ClawEval/GDPVal/SWE-bench Pro; 1000+ tool calls, native FP8, ~3x decode, harness awareness (self-manages context/memory toward the goal). Best for the hardest agentic SWE and harness self-management.
model_strengths.minimax-m3: MiniMax multimodal long-context coding agent (MSA sparse attn, 1M ctx). 59.0% SWE-Bench Pro, 66.0% Terminal-Bench 2.1, 74.2% MCP-Atlas, 83.5 BrowseComp; native image/video input + desktop computer-use; 55 Intelligence Index. Best for computer-use and 1M multimodal repos. COST: a 3x usage-VALUE deal is in effect now (3x the tokens per dollar), so effective cost is one-third of list (~$0.10 in / ~$0.40 out), NOT $1.20; that makes M3 currently the CHEAPEST mid model AND one of the strongest, an exceptional value, lean on it while it lasts. Caveat: weights/license pending.
model_strengths.minimax-m2.7: MiniMax text-only (~205K ctx). Cheap high-volume text agent. Best when you do not need M3's modalities or context.
model_strengths.minimax-m2.5: Prior MiniMax text generation; general cheap text reasoning. Fallback for pinned integrations.
model_strengths.qwen3.7-max: Alibaba agentic flagship, text-only, closed, 1M ctx. 69.7% Terminal-Bench 2.0-Terminus (beats DeepSeek-V4-Pro Max, Opus 4.6 Max, Kimi K2.6 Thinking); Anthropic-API compatible (Claude Code drop-in), 35h autonomous, 1000+ tool calls (internal claim). Top agentic-coding ceiling. Caveat: closed weights, priciest.
model_strengths.qwen3.7-plus: Alibaba balanced multimodal, closed, 1M ctx. 60% cheaper than Max; text+video+image. 70.3 Terminal-Bench 2.0-Terminus, 79.0 ScreenSpot Pro (UI grounding). Best cheap multimodal + GUI/screen understanding. Note: pricing roughly triples over 256K ctx.
model_strengths.qwen3.6-plus: Alibaba prior-gen Plus, closed. Stable integration target; 3.7 Plus is a drop-in id swap, so 3.6 Plus is the fallback if pinned. Note: pricing jumps over 256K ctx.
model_strengths.glm-5.1: Z.ai agentic engineering flagship (744B MoE / 40B active, MIT, trained on Huawei Ascend). 58.4 SWE-Bench Pro (tops the leaderboard past GPT-5.4 and Opus 4.6); runs an autonomous plan/execute/test/fix/optimize loop up to 8h. Best for long-horizon autonomous coding with open weights.
model_strengths.glm-5: Z.ai general open base (same 744B, MIT) that 5.1 post-trains from. First open-weights model to reach 50 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index. Strong general base; pick 5.1 for coding (5 to 5.1 is post-train only).
model_strengths.kimi-k2.6: Moonshot production-proven (1T MoE). Topped OpenRouter's weekly leaderboard on real API routing at launch. Best real-world adoption track record; stable drop-in if newer Kimi gains do not hold.
model_strengths.kimi-k2.5: Moonshot prior-gen Kimi (1T MoE); strong general/coding predecessor. Fallback for pinned integrations.
model_strengths.kimi-k2.7-code: Moonshot coding-agent specialist (1T MoE / 32B active, Modified MIT). 81.1 MCPMark Verified (beats Opus 4.8's 76.4, strongest open MCP tool-use); ~30% lower thinking-tokens vs K2.6, preserve-thinking across agent turns. Best for MCP/tool orchestration and token efficiency on long runs. Caveat: independent benchmarks being questioned; NOT yet in a tier or in opencode-go, confirm availability before routing to it.
model_strengths.qwen3.6-35b-a3b: Local (llama-swap), $0, open MoE. Sam's current daily driver: MoE efficiency, vision via mmproj, runs MXFP4 on the 5090. Per Sam's benchmarks MTP gives no net gain here, so non-MTP stays the daily driver. Only one local model resident at a time.
model_strengths.qwen3.6-27b: Local (llama-swap), $0, open dense. preserve_thinking baked in; dense arch shows ~1.49x MTP speedup in Sam's stack (vs the 35B-A3B MoE wash). Pick for draft-MTP workloads. Only one local model resident at a time.
model_strengths.diffusiongemma-26b-a4b: Google DeepMind text-DIFFUSION (26B MoE / 3.8B active, Apache 2.0), not autoregressive. 700+ tok/s on an RTX 5090 (~18GB quantized); 256 tokens generated in parallel per pass, every token attends to all, 256K ctx, multimodal in. Best for raw single-user throughput and inline editing/code infill on Sam's hardware. Caveat: underperforms Gemma 4 on MMLU/coding, experimental (speed over quality); NOT in a tier, local small-model zoo only.
model_strengths.opus: Anthropic reasoning flagship Opus 4.8 (1M ctx by default on API, 128K max output, adaptive thinking). Hardest reasoning, multi-file refactors, long-horizon agents; stronger agentic reliability + cheaper Fast mode than 4.7; accepts mid-conversation system messages without breaking prompt cache. S-tier, subscription/off-API-budget, NEVER a subagent.
model_strengths.gpt-5.5: OpenAI coding+reasoning flagship (Codex 5.5, 128K out). Improves on 5.4 with fewer tokens; strong at holding context across large systems, reasoning through ambiguous failures, tool-checked assumptions, carrying changes through a codebase; reasoning none/low/medium/high/xhigh; text+image. Top-tier agentic SWE in Codex (xhigh for hardest). S-tier. Caveat: not yet API-key routable in Codex (ChatGPT sign-in only); use gpt-5.4-codex for API-key flows.
model_strengths.gpt-5.4: OpenAI GPT-5.4 / Codex 5.4 coding specialist (1M ctx). Folds in 5.3-Codex's industry-leading coding plus improved tool/software-environment work; the API-key-routable Codex coding variant where 5.5 is not yet. A-tier.
model_strengths.sonnet: Anthropic Sonnet 4.6 daily-driver default (256K ctx; 1M available but NOT auto-upgraded, costs credits even on Max). 79.6% SWE-bench Verified, within 1.2 pts of Opus at ~40% less cost and faster output. Strong coding/writing/analysis/computer-use/vision/doc creation. Best value/throughput default unless a task outgrows it. A-tier.
model_strengths.haiku: Anthropic Haiku 4.5 fast/cheap tier (200K ctx, 64K out). ~97 tok/s; high-volume classification, routing, code review, extraction at scale; rivals Sonnet 4.0-level reasoning. Cheapest Claude per-token. B-tier.
model_strengths.gpt-5.1-mini: OpenAI GPT-5.1(-Codex)-Mini cheap fast coding mini (400K ctx, from $0.25/M in). Near-flagship coding/reasoning at low cost, vision, tool use. High-frequency subagent calls, interactive edits, latency/cost-sensitive coding. B-tier; legacy-tier now (newer is GPT-5.4-mini) but the price/context ratio holds.

attributes._note: Structured per-model routing attributes. The router scores on THESE, not on the model_strengths prose (prose is human docs only). grade = S/A/B/C/L quality axis that --difficulty matches against (S>A>B>C, L=local). modalities = hard-filterable input capabilities. ctx_max = hard context ceiling (candidate eliminated if contextTokens exceeds it). ctx_sweet_spot = soft: past this, quality/cost degrades and the candidate is penalized. roles = 0..1 affinity per orchestration role. traits = soft keyword bonuses. routable=false hard-eliminates (license/availability hold).

kimi-k2.7-code.routable_note: NOT yet in opencode-go and independent benchmarks questioned; flip to true once availability confirmed.

deepseek-4-flash._note: DigitalOcean's id for DeepSeek V4 Flash is literally 'deepseek-4-flash' (missing the v); same model as deepseek-v4-flash.

qwen3.5-397b-a17b._note: DigitalOcean large Qwen 3.5 MoE (397B/17B active); estimates, confirm.

reasoning._note: Reasoning the orchestrator applies AFTER the router picks a model, via the OpenCode model option reasoningEffort. VERIFIED in the opencode binary: OpenAIChat.lowerOptions emits reasoning_effort for OpenAI/DeepSeek/MiniMax and other openai-compatible providers; Anthropic maps reasoningEffort to an extended-thinking budget (low~16000, max~31999 budgetTokens). Allowed values are OpenCode's isReasoningEffort set: none, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh, max. by_difficulty maps the request --difficulty to a value; absent that, use default. effort 'auto' = do NOT set reasoningEffort (leave the model default); OpenCode rejects reasoningEffort on non-reasoning models.
reasoning._apply: Via Paseo (the normal path): pass create_agent settings.thinkingOptionId=<value>; Paseo maps it to the OpenCode model variant, the Claude SDK effort (thinking type adaptive), or the Codex reasoning_effort (verified paseo agent-manager.ts:1255 + per-provider agents). Standalone OpenCode CLI/config path: set options.reasoningEffort=<value> (lowered to reasoning_effort for openai-compatible, thinking budgetTokens for Anthropic). Skip entirely when value is 'auto'. OpenAI/Codex do NOT accept 'max'; DeepSeek does, and 'max' wants a large context window (>=384K) and generous output, and thinking ignores temperature.

minimax-m3._note: MiniMax M3 has a thinking/instant toggle at the MiniMax API, but OpenCode returns NO reasoningEffort variant for minimax (verified opencode transform.ts:646-658, returns {}), so it is NOT controllable via reasoningEffort through this stack; leave the model default.

sonnet._note: Paseo Claude provider thinking ids for Sonnet 4.6 are low/medium/high/max (verified models.ts:8-93); xhigh is Opus 4.7+/Fable only.

haiku._note: Paseo's Claude provider exposes NO thinking options for Haiku (verified models.ts:8-93); leave default.

speed._note: Relative responsiveness 0..1 (higher = faster, TTFT-oriented) used by --priority speed. ESTIMATES from model size/arch + vendor tok/s notes, NOT benchmarked on this stack; refine with measured TTFT when available. Mirrors DigitalOcean inference-router 'fastest' policy (selection by Time To First Token).

fallback._note: When a dispatched model fails, the orchestrator retries the next entry in the router's result.fallbacks chain ONLY for transient errors; on permanent errors it fails fast (retrying would just burn the chain). Match the provider error to these classes by status code or error name.

provider_priority._note: Per-SOURCE routing bonus added to a candidate's score so that, among providers serving the same model, the preferred source wins; the fallback chain then fails over to the rest. Reflects Sam's cost posture 2026-06: spend the free DigitalOcean GitHub Student credits (~$160) FIRST, then cheap cloud (Reasonix, OpenRouter), then free local, with opencode-go DEPRIORITIZED (usage running low) but kept as a last-resort fallback. Source is classified from the provider string: digitalocean, openrouter, reasonix, opencode-zen (the free Zen models), local (sam-desktop ==..==), local-edge (embedding box ==edge-..==), opencode-go, subscription. Tune freely.

permissions._note: Permission/sandbox posture per Paseo backend, applied at create_agent. DEFAULT is bypass/yolo (fully unattended) per operator policy. Backend = the provider-string prefix before the first slash: claude, codex, opencode, reasonix. The router emits the bypass settings for the chosen provider's backend; paseo-boo passes settings.modeId/features to create_agent. Verified against paseo list_providers 2026-06 (isUnattended modes) plus fork source; CLI flags are for the standalone CLI path.

opencode._note: build mode already defaults all tool permissions to allow (verified opencode agent.ts:106-164); residual asks are auto-approved by Paseo's create_agent features.auto_accept (a Paseo-layer feature, NOT an opencode config key). The standalone opencode CLI flag is --dangerously-skip-permissions (run.ts:231), which auto-approves ask results but still honors deny rules; there is no full bypass that overrides deny.

pricing._note: USD per 1M tokens, from Sam's 2026-06 pricing table. Primary cost tiebreaker when option-pool candidates fit comparably (output $/M dominates real cost). Local models (qwen3.6-*, diffusiongemma) are $0 self-host. _over_256k applies to the higher Qwen Plus context band.

kimi-k2.5._note: not in the 2026-06 table; assumed equal to K2.6, confirm

minimax-m3._note: 3x usage VALUE in effect NOW: 3x the tokens per dollar, so effective cost is one-third of list (~$0.10 in / ~$0.40 out). Router should cost M3 at the effective_* values; this makes it the CHEAPEST mid model currently. Drop the multiplier when the promo ends.

deepseek-v4-pro._note: Native DeepSeek API price (verified 2026-06; matches reasonix.example.toml prices block); the opencode-go/fireworks route was $1.74/$3.48. deepseek-v4-* now route via the Reasonix provider (reasonix/deepseek-pro/deepseek-v4-pro), which uses the same DeepSeek API and price.

gpt-5.5._note: S-tier; not yet API-key routable in Codex

haiku._note: B-tier; tier-list draft said $1/$15, using strength-sheet $1/$5, CONFIRM
